SUMMARY:
Looks fine to me, just a couple minor things.

POINTS:
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/steps/ThreadPoolSetup.java:33] It might be better to use `null` here and let the EQE's handler be the default.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/runtime/ExecutorRecorder.java:165] ...since you already `null`-check it here.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/runtime/ExecutorRecorder.java:184] ...which would make this unnecessary.
- [extensions/vertx-core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/core/runtime/VertxCoreRecorder.java:480] ```suggestion
                    vertxContext.beginDispatch();
                    try {
```

I'd do it like this, because you don't want `endDispatch` to run if `beginDispatch` failed for some reason.

POINTS:
- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoScan.java:145] It would be nice to do some sanity checks and I think those could be done here.  Could check for the following.

 * Error if `--csv` and `--json` are both set.
 * Error if `--csv-some-option` is set and `--csv` is not set.
 * Error if the delimiter, escape, or quote is more than one char.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:949] It occurred to me that the scan command is not part of Fluo's java API.   The scan command is outside of the Java API.  These configuration are related to Fluo's java API.  Based on this, I am thinking we should only have the command line options and not these configuration options.

It should be possible to use Fluo only via the Java API without ever using the Fluo tarball.  In this case, this scan configuration is out of place and confusing.

Is there a use case for having these config options that the command line options can not satisfy?
- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoScan.java:160] Why is this commented out?
- [modules/core/pom.xml:52] It would be nice if fluo core did not depend on these, and only fluo command depended on them.  Maybe fluo command code could optional pass a `Function<RowColumnValue, String>` to fluo core code.  This function could call commons-csv code and be defined in fluo command.  This function may be a horrible idea I have not given it a lot of thought.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:165] When I saw this `charAt(0)` it made me think of having the sanity check to ensure option only has one char.

POINTS:
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:185] As a TODO optimization: sizeClassed magazine group could be a whole different implementation which expect to only deal with size classes chunks and magazines which can have a more optimized logic and hoc behaviours e.g.
- chunks doesn't need a method to estimate the remaining capacity, since chunks could just have a read into method which return a boolean if successful instead of failing
- to verify: there is no need retire a chunk based on the remaining capacity because chunks will just always release from magazine if they cannot read "into" anything. This will give the current chunk the next allocation a chance to try allocate a new segment if released, without detaching/reattaching it
- to verify: maybe next in line is not even necessary since chunks are picked by the shared chunk queue (local or external) or allocated fresh new

The idea is to have much simple behaviour since we have allocations of fixed segment size and we don't care much about the size of allocation itself, since is decided upfront and fixed per size class
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:907] Not a Biggie but can be an atomic updater with a usedMemory volatile long field
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:946] Check first if owner thread is non null so can save reading the currentThread
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:992] I prefer having a field which is null instead of a potential bimorphic method call.
I will suggest a way to improve the benchmark to capture this.
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:1235] I prefer a field instead of a potential bimorphic method call just to know this information
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:1259] The offer to the local queue cannot fail so just make the return type to void
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:1729] I was wrong related next in line usage on size classed chunks: once it is deallocated because previously marked for deallocation and now fully refilled, it is placed as a candidate in next in line (local or thread local one), which is ok since or has it's full capacity back
- [buffer/src/main/java/io/netty/buffer/AdaptivePoolingAllocator.java:1894] We could decide to still use the local free list for shared magazines as well because the allocation path is guarded by a lock or just in exclusive mode.

In short we just need to check the local free list is not null here (but we need to modify the shared case to allocate it!)

SUMMARY:
@mvduijnhoven This PR looks really good! I just added a few comments.

POINTS:
- [README.md:102] Pls do not modify this list - it's handled by the all-contributors bot.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/SchemaGeneratorConfigCustomizer.java:19] There are two problems with this interface.

1. `io.quarkiverse.mcp.server.runtime` is not considered a public API so it should not be used outside this extesnion.
2. I don't think we want to make the `SchemaGeneratorConfigBuilder` part of our/MCP server public API. It's an implementation detail and we might want/need to change the library in the future.

I think that we should not allow users/extensions to customize the config for now.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/SchemaGeneratorProvider.java:21] Given the fact that we only use the `SchemaGenerator` in the tool manager, I'd place this logic directly in the constructor of the `ToolManagerImpl`.

Your approach is fine but it produces two CDI bean definitions that are useless.

Also there's again a problem with having a `SchemaGenerator` part of the public API...
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/config/McpServerSchemaGeneratorJacksonRuntimeConfig.java:8] ```suggestion
@ConfigMapping(prefix = "quarkus.mcp.server.schema-generator.jackson")
```
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/config/McpServerSchemaGeneratorJakartaValidationRuntimeConfig.java:8] ```suggestion
@ConfigMapping(prefix = "quarkus.mcp.server.schema-generator.jakarta-validation")
```
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/config/McpServerSchemaGeneratorSwagger2RuntimeConfig.java:8] ```suggestion
@ConfigMapping(prefix = "quarkus.mcp.server.schema-generator.swagger2")
```
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/SchemaGeneratorConfigCustomizerJackson.java:14] These customizers represent one-off actions so I'd use the `@Dependent` pseudoscope instead - there's no need to keep them around for the lifecycle of the app.

You could also make the class package-private.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/deployment/JsonSchemaGeneratorProcessor.java:23] I think that we don't need to index these dependencies but explore the runtime dependencies instead.

Maybe consume the `CurateOutcomeBuildItem` and then use the `curateOutcomeBuildItem.getApplicationModel().getRuntimeDependencies()` instead.

Also there is no need to produce those `FeatureBuildItem`s.
- [core/runtime/pom.xml:32] As I commented on the `JsonSchemaGeneratorProcessor` - these three dependencies should not be here at all.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/deployment/JsonSchemaGeneratorProcessor.java:39] Also it should not be necessary to use `AdditionalBeanBuildItem.unremovableOf()` because the impls are injected in the `ToolManagerImpl`.

Hi, I submitted this PR to solve #28926.

I'm not sure if `MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException` should inherit `ApplicationContextException` and if `MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException` should be thrown upwards.

+/**
+ * Throws exception when web server factory bean is missing.
+ *
+ * @author Guirong Hu

+ */
+@SuppressWarnings("serial")
+public class MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException extends ApplicationContextException {
+
+	private final WebApplicationType webApplicationType;
+
+	/**
+	 * Create a new {@code MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException} with the given web
+	 * application context class and the given web server factory class and the given type
+	 * of web application.
+	 * @param webApplicationContextClass the web application context class
+	 * @param webServerFactoryClass the web server factory class
+	 * @param webApplicationType the type of web application
+	 */
+	public MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException(@NonNull Class<?> webApplicationContextClass,
+			@NonNull Class<?> webServerFactoryClass, @NonNull WebApplicationType webApplicationType) {
+		super(String.format("Unable to start %s due to missing %s bean.", webApplicationContextClass.getSimpleName(),
+				webServerFactoryClass.getSimpleName()));
+		this.webApplicationType = webApplicationType;
+	}
+

SUMMARY:
Thanks very much for the PR, @terminux. I've left some comments for your consideration when you have time.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:35] I don't think this will work. You could have a dependency on `spring-webmvc` but no Jetty, Tomcat, or Undertow dependency and you'd get a failure due to a missing web server factory bean.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:37] This has a similar problem. You could have a dependency on `spring-webflux` but no Jetty, Reactor Netty, Tomcat, or Undertow dependency and you'd get a failure due to a missing web server factory bean.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:37] This has a similar problem. You could have a dependency on `spring-webflux` but no Jetty, Reactor Netty, Tomcat, or Undertow dependency and you'd get a failure due to a missing web server factory bean.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:63] I think it would be better to avoid the `webApplicationType != null` check here by requiring the exception to have a non-null type. I also don't think the check for `NONE` should be necessary as this failure should never occur in that case.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:63] I think it would be better to avoid the `webApplicationType != null` check here by requiring the exception to have a non-null type.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:86] Being specific here will be tricky (see my comments above about the indicator classes). I think we may need to keep the actions fairly general to avoid making suggestions that potentially won't help.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:86] Being specific here will be tricky (see my comments above about the indicator classes). I think it might be better to display a more general action that recommends checking the configuration of the `web-application-type` property and the application's dependencies for a supported web server.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/reactive/context/ReactiveWebServerApplicationContext.java:110] As we now have a specific exception, we have the opportunity to ensure that the message is consistent. To that end, I wonder if `ReactiveWebApplicationContext.class` and `ReactiveWebServerFactory.class` (or `String` equivalents) should be passed to the constructor and it can then create the message.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/reactive/context/ReactiveWebServerApplicationContext.java:110] As we now have a specific exception, we have the opportunity to ensure that the message is specific. To that end, I wonder if `ReactiveWebApplicationContext.class` and `ReactiveWebServerFactory.class` (or `String`) equivalents should be passed to the constructor and it can then create the message.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/server/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException.java:29] I think it would be better to extend `ApplicationContextException`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/server/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException.java:29] I think it would be better to extend `ApplicationContextException`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/server/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException.java:50] This exception will never have a cause as currently used so I think we could remove this constructor for now at least.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/server/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException.java:50] This exception will never have a cause as currently used so I think we could remove this constructor for now at least.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/server/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException.java:17] I'm not sure where it should go instead, but this package doesn't feel right to me as the failure is specific to the web server being used in an application context. I'm not sure it's worth creating a `org.springframework.boot.web.server.context` just for this, and the two existing `context` packages are Servlet and reactive-specific.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/server/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanException.java:17] I'm not sure where it should go instead, but this package doesn't feel right to me as the failure is specific to the web server being used in an application context. I'm not sure it's worth creating a `org.springframework.boot.web.server.context` just for this, and the two existing `context` packages are Servlet and reactive-specific.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:17] I'll need to double-check, but this might cause a package tangle. I think it would probably be better if the analyzer went in the same package as the new exception.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/diagnostics/analyzer/MissingWebServerFactoryBeanFailureAnalyzer.java:17] I'll need to double-check, but this might cause a package tangle. I think it would probably be better if the analyzer went in the same package as the new exception.

SUMMARY:
I have also tested with Snippet389 (with both raster images at 100% and 200% as well as with SVGs) and found everything to work fine. Still I think the current error calculation is not correct and with that the overall calculation of the `srcPixels`.
Apart from that, I only have comments regarding code style.

POINTS:
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/GC.java:1239] Shouldn't we just calculate the scale factor in both axes? Some images (like SVGs or based on ImageDataAtSizeProvider) are able to scale both axes differently.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/GC.java:1227] With the adaptations in this PR, I think it would clearer if the `scaledImageZoom` was calculated in this method based on the `imageZoom` instead of passing it this method and calculate it out of the `gcZoom` (which is hard to comprehend).
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/GC.java:1241] Why do we change this calculation? Previously, `src` was used instead of `srcPixels` and compared against `b`, which seems to have been the bounds in pixels. That does not sound correct. Is that the reason for changing it? But since the `srcPixels` might be different later (depending on what handle we get), this new calculation is not correct either, is it?
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/GC.java:1260] Can't we just drop this if block, as the else block will yield the same result (just with a slight calculation overhead that will not be relevant in the overall computation time)? That would simplify the method signature and the intellectual load of understanding the behavior. It might also relieved us from calculating the `srcPixels` in the calling method at all, as in my understanding the usage of that value is not correct anyway (see my other comment on that).

Since the actual image zoom and thus the src coordinates in pixels always depend on the handle that the image will give us for the requested width/height, it would, in my opinion, be conceptually the right think to only calculate the srcPixels based on the handle once we have it.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @asashour. I've left a few comments for your consideration when you have a minute.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/embedded/jetty/JettyWebServer.java:171] I think it might be nice if the use of `connectors` was kept encapsulated in `getActualPortsDescription()`. Perhaps that method could be changed to return strings like "port 8080 (http)" or "ports 8080 (http), 8443 (https)"
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/embedded/tomcat/TomcatWebServer.java:108] Similar to my comment about Jetty, it would be good to keep the use of the connectors encapsulated in `getPortsDescription`. An added benefit would be that they would only be found once then too.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/embedded/undertow/UndertowWebServer.java:318] Same comment again.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/web/embedded/tomcat/TomcatWebServer.java:109] Tomcat's the only case where we use a `:`. As part of this, it would be good to make that consistent. I'm inclined to remove it from Tomcat's log message rather than adding one to the other servers' messages.

POINTS:
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/delete/PageFilter.java:20] interface
We avoid ABC for code reuse
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/delete/DeleteManager.java:117] Instead of creating a nested tree out of these ANDs, I would create a flattened list and compose filter from that
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/delete/PageFilter.java:22] I would define a mini-version of io.trino.operator.project.SelectedPositions and use that here.
The range version can be used to avoid the awkwardness of defining fake list for filtering.
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/delete/PageFilter.java:47] RowTest -> PositionFilter
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/delete/EqualityDeleteFilter.java:80] Can LazyTrinoRow be deleted now ?
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/delete/EqualityDeleteFilter.java:54] We were relying on hash and comparator from iceberg StructLikeWrapper before, does it matter that we're switching to Trino's internal hash code and comparison operators now ?
- [core/trino-spi/src/main/java/io/trino/spi/FlatHashStrategyFactory.java:23] I don't think we need to expose entire FlatHashStrategy, define a narrow interface for exactly what you need.
See io.trino.spi.PageSorter for example

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/observer/MetricsReporter.java] Wondering if it would be better to put this in a metrics package in the API. We may want to expose metrics in other places than Observer in the future.  For example, FluoClient could have a getMetrics call possibly in the future and/or the Loader.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/observer/MetricsReporter.java] I can't really remember what a meter is.... this probably needs more javadoc explaining what a meter is.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/observer/MetricsReporter.java] Do different metrics need unique names?  Is it ok for a counter and meter to have the same name?
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/observer/MetricsReporter.java] Was thinking about API and was wondering if it would be better to structure API like following.

``` java
MetricsReporter reporter = ...
Counter counter = reporter.counter("foo");
counter.update(9);
```

This allows potential to keep a refernce to a metric for repeated use and avoid looking up the metric by name.

pulsar-client-api-v5/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/api/v5/async/AsyncTransaction.java Diff (+5048/-0): PR DESCRIPTION: ## Summary Implementation of [PIP-466: New Java Client API (V5)](https://github.com/apache/pulsar/pull/25455) for scalable topic support. - Introduces the `pulsar-client-api-v5` module with type-safe interfaces for scalable topics - **Producer**: `Producer` / `ProducerBuilder` with key-based routing, flow control, and async variants - **StreamConsumer**: exclusive segment assignment with key-ordered delivery, controller-coordinated rebalancing - **QueueConsumer**: shared dispatch across all segments, no controller required - **CheckpointConsumer**: externalized position tracking via serializable `Checkpoint` snapshots for stream processing frameworks (Flink, Beam) - **PulsarClient / PulsarClientBuilder**: entry point with ServiceLoader-based provider discovery - Async counterparts (`AsyncProducer`, `AsyncStreamConsumer`, `AsyncQueueConsumer`, `AsyncCheckpointConsumer`) for non-blocking usage - Schema, auth, config, and internal provider packages - Comprehensive usage examples in `Examples.java` Related PIPs: - [PIP-460: Scalable Topics](https://github.com/apache/pulsar/blob/master/pip/pip-460.md) - [PIP-466: New Java Client API (V5)](https://github.com/apache/pulsar/pull/25455) ## Test plan - [x] Module SUMMARY:
I added a few review comments

POINTS:
- [pulsar-client-api-v5/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/api/v5/auth/AuthenticationFactory.java:92] These should be async methods since creating the authentication could involve IO. There's https://github.com/apache/pulsar/blob/master/pip/pip-97.md about this.
- [pulsar-client-api-v5/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/api/v5/schema/Schema.java:192] Should use `com.google.protobuf.Message` instead of `com.google.protobuf.GeneratedMessageV3`:

```
- [pulsar-client-api-v5/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/api/v5/PulsarClient.java:90] Does creating a transaction contain IO operations? if it does, shouldn't this return `CompletableFuture<Transaction>`?
- [pulsar-client-api-v5/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/api/v5/PulsarClient.java:90] Would it be useful to return a `TransactionBuilder` instead, just in case creating a transaction contains configurable parameters later?
- [pulsar-client-api-v5/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/api/v5/PulsarClient.java:114] Would it be useful to also add closeAsync?
